parameters.content:
### Every Few Minutes

Starts the workflow on a timer — every 10 min. No input data; downstream nodes fetch what they need.

Next: Fetch Current Metrics

### Fetch Current Metrics

GET https://api.example.com/v1/assets — retries 3× with backoff on failure.

Ports: ①→ Compute Severity Signals · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 API key / bearer for the target API

### Compute Severity Signals

Custom JavaScript: Compute a simple moving average and momentum signal

Next: Grade Severity Tier

### Grade Severity Tier

Routes each item to one of 4 lanes: info, warning, critical + fallback.

Ports: ①→ Just Record (Info) · ②→ Warn Channel (Outlook) · ③→ Page Urgently (Slack) · ④→ Archive Unscored

### Just Record (Info)

googleSheets — `append` operation against the connected account.

Ports: ①→ Merge Tier Outcomes · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Warn Channel (Outlook)

microsoftOutlook — `action` operation against the connected account.

Ports: ①→ Merge Tier Outcomes · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Microsoft OAuth2

### Page Urgently (Slack)

slack — `action` operation against the connected account.

Ports: ①→ Merge Tier Outcomes · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

### Archive Unscored

googleSheets — `append` operation against the connected account.

Ports: ①→ Merge Tier Outcomes · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Merge Tier Outcomes

Rejoins 4 branches into one stream (mode: append) so downstream logic runs once.

Next: Align Field Names

### Align Field Names

Normalizes field names so every source feeds the same downstream schema.

Next: Stamp Processing Time

### Stamp Processing Time

Stamps `processedAt` with the current time for auditing.

Next: Send Summary to Outlook

### Send Summary to Outlook

microsoftOutlook — `action` operation against the connected account.

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Microsoft OAuth2

### Capture Failure Context

Shapes the item: writes `failedNode`, `errorMessage`, `workflow`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Escalate Failure to Outlook

### Escalate Failure to Outlook

microsoftOutlook — `action` operation against the connected account.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Microsoft OAuth2

# Monitor a key metric with HTTP / REST API and Outlook 24/7

**Pattern:** Threshold Monitoring · **Level:** Core · **Trigger:** Scheduled

## What it does
Monitor a key metric with HTTP / REST API and Outlook 24/7. It runs automatically on a schedule.

## Why it exists
Manual risk monitoring is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
Every Few Minutes → Fetch Current Metrics → Compute Severity Signals → Grade Severity Tier → Just Record (Info) → Merge Tier Outcomes → Align Field Names → …

## Use it for
- Teams that want to threshold monitoring without manual effort
- Anyone automating risk monitoring in finance
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Keeps your systems in sync
- Improves response time
- Saves hours of manual work each week

## Before first run
Connect credentials: API key / bearer for the target API; Google OAuth2 / service account; Microsoft OAuth2; Slack OAuth2.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## 🛟 Error handling & retries

External calls retry **3× with 2s backoff**. Anything still failing exits through its node's error output into the red error rail → context is captured and the on-call channel is alerted. Set an n8n **Error Workflow** in settings as the final safety net.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your risk monitoring process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.
